hero.title: RF Online Next Wiki
hero.description: RF ONLINE NEXT is a cross-platform sci-fi MMORPG with Biosuit class swapping, Sacred Weapons, MAU and Animus combat, world boss raids, massive PvP, and PC/mobile play.

gameFeature.title: RF Online Next Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Master Biosuit class swapping, Sacred Weapons like MAU and Animus, world boss raids, and massive PvP in this cross-platform sci-fi MMORPG.

rfOnlineNextDownloadPlatforms.title: RF Online Next Download, Release Date and Platforms
rfOnlineNextDownloadPlatforms.intro: RF Online Next launched worldwide on June 16, 2026 for PC and mobile. Pick one official platform to install the real game and skip third-party APK mirrors or copied launcher pages.

cards.name: Official Site and Netmarble Launcher
cards.description: Official global download hub for the Netmarble Launcher PC version with Netmarble Connect remote play support.

cards.name: Epic Games Store
cards.description: Free base game on the Epic Games Store. Cross Platform, MMO, Open World, RPG, and Space.

cards.name: Google Play
cards.description: Official Android install page with 500K+ downloads. Role-playing MMORPG with competitive multiplayer.

cards.name: App Store
cards.description: Official iPhone and iPad install page. Requires iOS 15.0 or later. Version 1.27.12, 392.5 MB.

cards.name: PC System Requirements
cards.description: Minimum specs start at an Intel i5 8400, 12 GB RAM, and GTX 1060. Recommended steps up to an i5 12400, 16 GB, and RTX 2060.

cards.name: Supported Languages
cards.description: Audio in Korean and English. Text support spans 16 languages including Simplified and Traditional Chinese, French, German, and Japanese.

rfOnlineNextCodesRewards.title: RF Online Next Codes and Coupon Rewards
rfOnlineNextCodesRewards.intro: Track the official coupon page, launch rewards, Twitch Drops, Discord events, and partner campaigns. Coupon rewards are tied to your account membership number and delivered to the in-game mailbox.

cards.name: Official Coupon Center
cards.reward: Exclusive coupon rewards delivered to your linked account mailbox
cards.description: Enter the coupon on Netmarble's RF Online Next coupon page and confirm your account membership number before redeeming.
cards.note: One redemption per account. Restart the game and recheck the mailbox if the reward is delayed.

cards.name: Twitch Drops Event
cards.reward: Twitch Drops for watching official RF Online Next streams
cards.description: Log into your game account first, then log into Twitch and watch eligible RF Online Next streams.
cards.note: Event runs June 17 to June 30, 2026 14:59 UTC.

cards.name: App Marketplace Pre-registration Rewards
cards.reward: Rare Biosuit Summon Ticket, Uncommon Rover summon tickets, and Combat Support Packages
cards.description: Pre-registration through Google Play or the App Store qualified your account for launch rewards.
cards.note: Delivered via in-game mail at launch.

cards.name: Official Website Email Rewards
cards.reward: Epic Rover Flora Summon Chest, in-game credits, upgrade kits, and progression items
cards.description: Email pre-registration through the official website qualified your account for the website reward set.
cards.note: Delivered through the game account reward system.

cards.name: Discord Invite Event
cards.description: Join the RF Online Next Official Discord and follow the invite event instructions from the official forum notice.
cards.note: Announced May 28, 2026. Remain in the server until rewards are distributed.

cards.name: RF Online Next x GearUP Partnership
cards.description: Use the official Netmarble forum event notice and follow the GearUP campaign steps listed there.

rfOnlineNextBeginnerGuide.title: RF Online Next Beginner Guide
rfOnlineNextBeginnerGuide.intro: Follow the early path from account setup to flight, Biosuit equipping, Sacred Weapon deployment, level 30, and regional main quests. Keep the first hour simple and stay on official platforms.

steps.title: Install from an official platform
steps.subtitle: Netmarble Launcher, Epic Games Store, Google Play, or App Store
steps.description: PC players install through Netmarble Launcher or Epic Games Store. Mobile players use Google Play or the App Store so the account and purchase flow stay inside the official ecosystem.

steps.title: Create your game account and character
steps.subtitle: Pick your region, then the character you will keep
steps.description: Choose the same server as friends or guildmates before committing early progress, because MMO server choice affects who you can reliably play with.

steps.title: Set up PC controls before fighting
steps.subtitle: WASD to move, Space Bar to fly
steps.description: Alt + Enter or F11 toggles full screen and windowed mode. Adjust graphics quality from Settings then Environment.

steps.title: Reach level 5
steps.subtitle: Clear the opening quests
steps.description: The Great Beginning achievement marks level 5, the first clean early-progression checkpoint in RF Online Next.

steps.title: Finish the Biosuit Flight Tutorial
steps.subtitle: Complete the Colleague in Danger quest
steps.description: This unlock path teaches the flight system, which matters because Biosuits fight across both air and ground.

steps.title: Complete the Biosuit Equipping Tutorial
steps.subtitle: Complete the Bellato's Ammunition Depot quest
steps.description: Your first major step into RF Online Next's class-switching system, where Biosuits change combat style without restarting the character.

steps.title: Clear the Ancient Ruins objective
steps.subtitle: Defeat the Holy Stone Keeper
steps.description: This early boss checkpoint confirms your character can handle tutorial combat, movement, and objective progression.

steps.title: Deploy Sacred Weapons
steps.subtitle: Deploy Sacred Weapons five times
steps.description: Sacred Weapons include battlefield tools such as the Launcher, MAU, and Animus, central to RF Online Next's large-scale combat identity.

steps.title: Push to level 30 and regional main quests
steps.subtitle: Head toward Dorian, Numerus, and Kallis
steps.description: Valiant Mercenary marks level 30. Regional achievements track the Bellato, Cora, and Accretia quest lines.

quickTips: Always install RF Online Next from an official platform to keep your account safe.
Match your server choice with friends and guildmates early.
Finish the flight and Biosuit tutorials before deep PvE or PvP.
Save Sacred Weapon deployments for the fights that need them most.

rfOnlineNextBiosuitTierList.title: RF Online Next Class Tier List and Biosuits
rfOnlineNextBiosuitTierList.intro: Biosuits are RF Online Next's class system, supporting flight, air-and-ground combat, and on-the-fly role switching. This tier grid ranks Biosuits for new-player comfort and practical early usefulness, while showing what each suit does in PvE and large-scale PvP.

biosuits.name: Demolisher
biosuits.pve: Mob clearing, field farming, dungeon waves
biosuits.note: Built around heavy weaponry and area damage, the cleanest first pick for strong output without standing in melee.

biosuits.name: Punisher
biosuits.role: Stable ranged damage and ally cover
biosuits.pvp: Cover fire, crowd control, ranged finishing
biosuits.note: Focuses on bullets, flames, and covering allies, a safer damage style before full flight and PvP positioning.

biosuits.name: Enforcer
biosuits.pve: Survival, multi-enemy pulls, safer boss learning
biosuits.note: Engages multiple enemies and blocks attacks, ideal for durability and team protection.

biosuits.name: Dreadnought
biosuits.pvp: Chasing targets, combo pressure, frontline damage
biosuits.note: For players who charge in and stay aggressive, though melee positioning is less forgiving than ranged starters.

biosuits.name: Psypher
biosuits.note: Uses Force to control fights and handle multiple enemies, strong once you understand skill timing.

biosuits.name: Technician
biosuits.note: Important in party battles, but solo beginners usually progress faster with a damage Biosuit first.

biosuits.name: Phantom
biosuits.note: Speed and assassination value, but it punishes poor timing and positioning.

biosuits.name: Arbiter
biosuits.pve: Hybrid damage routes, flexible quest combat
biosuits.note: A flexible combat pick rather than the simplest starter, because hybrid range asks for careful positioning.

rfOnlineNextBuildProgression.title: RF Online Next Best Builds, Skills and Gear Progression
rfOnlineNextBuildProgression.intro: RF Online Next progression is built around flexible Biosuit swapping instead of a one-class lock-in. The safest build plan is to keep one main combat role strong, unlock practical swap options, and fix gear and passive stat gaps before spreading upgrade resources across every shiny menu button the game throws at you.

steps.title: Choose one main damage Biosuit first
steps.summary: Start by investing around the Biosuit you actually use for main quests, dungeons and boss contribution.
details: Each Biosuit has unique effects, and owned Biosuits can be swapped freely from the Biosuit menu.
Firepower suits such as Demolisher and Punisher are practical mid-to-long-range physical attack anchors for early solo progress.
Treat other Biosuits as swap tools until your main route has stable gear, skills and passive stats.

steps.title: Use swapping for roles, not random spending
steps.summary: Biosuit swapping lets you change combat style for the situation while keeping equipment and skill growth relevant.
details: Use a damage suit for quest clearing and routine farming.
Keep a defensive, evasive or support-leaning swap option ready for PvP, party play and awkward boss patterns.
Avoid splitting upgrade materials evenly across every Biosuit early.

steps.title: Fix gear enhancement before chasing side systems
steps.summary: When main quest progress slows or monsters start hitting too hard, upgrade equipped gear before blaming the class.
details: Equipment Enhancement includes Enhance, Batch Enhance, Prime Mod and Random Option layers.
Early bottlenecks are usually solved faster by bringing active equipment through safe enhancement than by gambling on a new setup.
Use the Equipment Enhancement menu to keep your active route upgraded before pushing lower-impact side growth.

steps.title: Raise skill power on the skills you actually cast
steps.summary: Skill growth matters because the game supports Adding Skills, Skill Enhancements, Add-Ons and Finishing Skills.
details: Prioritize the active skills used by your main Biosuit route.
Add-ons should support your job in that fight: damage uptime for farming, control or survival for PvP, and burst windows for bosses.
Do not overbuild unused skills just because the menu has a red dot.

steps.title: Complete collection stats with attack and accuracy first
steps.summary: Collection progress gives passive stat value, and attack plus accuracy are the cleanest early priorities for damage consistency.
details: Accuracy matters because missed or unreliable hits waste every other damage upgrade.
After attack and accuracy, branch into durability, evasion or role-specific stats based on your bottleneck.

steps.title: Use Rovers as passive growth, not decoration
steps.summary: Rovers function like floating companion units and can provide practical stat and utility bonuses.
details: Rover systems include Rover, Fuse, Collection, Finalize, Tuning, Rover Workshop and Rover Burst.
Rover bonuses include growth-friendly effects such as EXP gain, healing item capacity and attack-related stats.
Keep Rover growth aligned with your main build instead of treating companions as cosmetic extras.

rfOnlineNextSacredWeapons.title: RF Online Next Sacred Weapons Guide
rfOnlineNextSacredWeapons.intro: Sacred Weapons are not normal gear slots. They are battlefield-scale tools built for timing, positioning and objective pressure, especially when large-scale PvP, boss raids and aerial combat decide a fight.

cards.name: MAU
cards.description: MAU stands for Mechanic Armor Unit, a large-scale weapon that players directly pilot and control.
bestUsed: Creating a frontline threat in large engagements
cards.playNote: Use MAU when direct pressure matters more than subtle positioning.

cards.name: Launcher
cards.role: Precise ranged firepower and defensive break pressure
cards.description: Launcher unleashes precise, devastating firepower and helps obliterate enemy defenses or cut off retreat routes.
cards.playNote: Deploy Launcher where its firepower can cover a lane, objective or retreat path instead of wasting it in empty space.

cards.name: Animus
cards.role: Large-scale burst presence and battlefield control
cards.description: Animus is an immense and powerful Sacred Weapon that can be summoned to seize control of the battlefield.
bestUsed: Adding decisive pressure when a group fight stalls
cards.playNote: Save Animus-style pressure for moments that decide the fight rather than disposable skirmishes.

cards.name: Sacred Weapon Deployment
cards.description: The game's achievements include Steely Watcher for completing Sacred Weapon Deployment five times.
bestUsed: Building comfort before large PvP fights
cards.playNote: Use repeated deployments as practice for timing, not just as a checkbox.

rfOnlineNextPvpWarfare.title: RF Online Next PvP, Battlegrounds and Mining War
rfOnlineNextPvpWarfare.intro: RF Online Next turns PvP into a layered war system instead of a simple duel. Know the objective, swap your Biosuit for the fight, control vertical space, and keep the battlefield mechanics in mind while chasing a coordinated push.

items.label: What is the core PvP fantasy?
items.content: RF Online Next is built around massive PvP where thousands of players clash and faction honor is on the line. The game emphasizes large combat swings, battlefield positioning and victory through coordinated pressure rather than isolated one-on-one fights.
keyPoints: Large-scale PvP is a central feature
Faction conflict drives the battlefield fantasy
Sacred Weapons and flight can disrupt enemy strategy

items.label: How does Biosuit swapping affect team fights?
items.content: Biosuits can be swapped to change combat style for the situation. Switching between offense, evasion and support roles makes team fights less about one fixed class and more about bringing the right role at the right moment.
keyPoints: Swap for offense when your team is pushing
Swap for evasion when repositioning or disengaging
Swap for support when group survival matters

items.label: What matters in Mining War or Faction War objectives?
items.content: Faction War centers on excavators. Players can approach an excavator to view its faction and HP, and the excavator loses HP by number of hits rather than damage value. Destroying all opposing excavators wins the war; if excavators remain, the guild from the faction with the highest remaining excavator HP wins.
keyPoints: Excavator HP is reduced by hit count
Destroy opposing excavators to win
Remaining excavator HP can decide the winning faction

items.label: Why is the Holy Stone Keeper important?
items.content: The Holy Stone Keeper spawns in the center one minute after the Faction War starts and respawns three minutes after defeat. Defeating it can grant a barrier to the faction's excavator, and the faction that lands the final hit receives a buff that scales through movement speed, attack, defense, accuracy and evasion tiers.
keyPoints: First spawn: one minute after war start
Respawn: three minutes after defeat
Final-hit faction receives escalating combat buffs

items.label: How does flight change battleground movement?
items.content: The airfield opens ten minutes after the game begins, allowing movement between Accretia, Bellato and Cora airfields. Flight creates flanking routes and vertical pressure, but flying too high can cause fuel shortage and death.
keyPoints: Airfield opens after ten minutes
Faction airfields connect to enemy airfields
Fuel limits prevent careless high-altitude flight

items.label: What do turrets, barricades and guardians do?
items.content: When a faction defeats the Holy Stone Keeper, that faction's turrets activate. Turrets can destroy opposing barricades, opening easier attack paths toward enemy bases, while guardians near entrances and excavators help defend against intrusions.
keyPoints: Holy Stone Keeper control activates turrets
Turrets are used to break barricades
Guardians support base defense

items.label: How do mercenaries fit into the war?
items.content: The Mercenary System lets guild leaders set recruitment criteria and prize rewards for participating mercenaries. Recruitment is available until 21:10, prize money cannot be changed from 21:25 onward, and the listed prize range is 100 to 100,000 Diamonds.
keyPoints: Guild Master controls mercenary recruitment settings
Recruitment cutoff is 21:10
Prize range is 100 to 100,000 Diamonds

rfOnlineNextPveRoadmap.title: RF Online Next World Bosses, Dungeons and Main Quests
rfOnlineNextPveRoadmap.intro: This roadmap turns RF Online Next's early progression into practical checkpoints. It uses achievement goals, guide categories and boss rules so players can understand what to clear next without reading every menu.

rows.goal: Complete opening quests and unlock the first basic systems.
rows.reward: The Great Beginning achievement and early account XP

rows.milestone: Complete the Colleague in Danger Quest
rows.goal: Learn flight movement and aerial control for travel and combat positioning.

rows.milestone: Complete the Bellato's Ammunition Depot Quest
rows.goal: Equip and understand the Biosuit system before deeper combat routing.

rows.milestone: Defeat the Holy Stone Keeper in the Ancient Ruins
rows.goal: Clear the Arcane Ruins Quest and learn objective-style boss pressure.
Keep main quests, gear enhancement and routine content moving until mid-game systems open wider.

rows.milestone: Complete Sacred Weapon Deployment five times
rows.goal: Practice deploying MAU, Launcher or Animus-style battlefield tools before relying on them in bigger fights.

rows.milestone: Complete the Dorian Regional Main Quest
rows.goal: Finish the Bellato-linked regional story route.

rows.milestone: Complete the Kallis Regional Main Quest
rows.goal: Finish the Accretia-linked regional story route.

rows.milestone: Complete the Numerus Regional Main Quest
rows.goal: Finish the Cora-linked regional story route.

rows.milestone: Check boss spawn region and timing in the Schedule menu
rows.goal: Stay near the boss, deal contribution, and avoid changing maps during the fight.

rows.milestone: Compete as an individual, party or raid-group party
rows.goal: Earn contribution and keep positioning valid until the boss dies.

rows.milestone: Scion Training Grounds, World Boss, Zone Dungeon, Core World, Arcane Trials, Battlefield of Memory, Babylon Occupation Battle
rows.goal: Use dungeon categories to route daily growth, boss attempts and harder group content.
rows.reward: Progression materials, boss rewards and long-term PvE goals

rows.goal: Continue main progression, dungeons, boss content and gear growth toward the high-level goal.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about RF Online Next

questions.question: What is RF Online Next?
questions.answer: RF Online Next is Netmarble's cross-platform sci-fi MMORPG, built around Biosuit class switching, Sacred Weapons, world boss raids, and large-scale PvP across PC and mobile.

questions.question: Where can I download RF Online Next?
questions.answer: Download RF Online Next from the official Netmarble site, Epic Games Store, Google Play, or the App Store. Use these official platforms to keep your account safe.

questions.question: Is RF Online Next free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. RF Online Next is free-to-play on PC and mobile with optional in-app purchases for cosmetics and progression items.

questions.question: How do I redeem RF Online Next coupon codes?
questions.answer: Enter coupons on Netmarble's official RF Online Next coupon page, confirm your account membership number, and rewards are delivered to your in-game mailbox.

questions.question: Does RF Online Next support cross-platform play?
questions.answer: Yes. RF Online Next supports cross-platform play between PC and mobile, so you can progress the same account across devices.

questions.question: What are Biosuits in RF Online Next?
questions.answer: Biosuits are RF Online Next's class system. They support flight, air-and-ground combat, and let you switch combat roles without restarting your character.
